title: The Seventeenth Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
September 15, 2024 | Preacher: Katie Pearson

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Seventeenth Sunday after Pentecost in Ordinary Time, placing the church in the long green season between Pentecost and Advent. The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this Sunday (Year B) would likely include Proverbs 1:20-33, Psalm 19, James 3:1-12, and Mark 8:27-38—focusing on themes of wisdom, James's teaching on the power of the tongue, and Peter's confession of Jesus followed by Jesus's first prediction of his passion. The emphasis falls on discipleship, suffering, and the cost of following Christ.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the immediate aftermath of General Convention 2024, held in Louisville, Kentucky in June, which had passed several significant resolutions on marriage equality, reparations, and restructuring. The Diocese of Colorado, led by Bishop Kimberly Chastain (elected 2022), was navigating post-Convention implementation. No major Episcopal controversies or bishop elections were actively dominating headlines in mid-September 2024, though the church remained in a period of significant doctrinal and structural transition.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing early fall weather patterns typical for September, with generally mild conditions. The city was in the lead-up to the fall election cycle, with Colorado's statewide elections scheduled for November 2024. Local news was largely focused on education issues, as schools had returned to session in early September. No major weather emergencies or catastrophic events were reported in the immediate week before this sermon.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The 2024 presidential campaign was in its final eight weeks, with Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Donald Trump locked in a competitive general election race following President Biden's withdrawal in late July. Hurricane Helene was developing in the Atlantic during mid-September and would make landfall on September 26, becoming a major disaster story. The Federal Reserve was managing inflation and interest rate decisions in an uncertain economic environment, and debates over reproductive rights and immigration remained central to campaign messaging.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Ukraine-Russia conflict continued with active fighting in eastern Ukraine and ongoing questions about Western military aid. The Middle East remained tense following the anniversary of October 7, 2023, with discussions ongoing about humanitarian conditions in Gaza. Climate and environmental stories were building momentum ahead of the UN Climate Action Summit (COP29) scheduled for November in Baku, Azerbaijan.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in mid-September 2024 was characterized by election anxiety and polarization, with the presidential race consuming significant media and social attention. The autumn cultural calendar was beginning with new television and film releases, and sports were in full swing with NFL football (Denver Broncos season underway), MLB heading toward playoffs, and college football dominant. There was a general sense of uncertainty about the near-term political future combined with the cyclical rhythm of fall routines and the return to school and work structures.
